fundamentals.name: Lumentum Holdings Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. manufactures and sells optical and photonic products in the Americas, the Asia-Pacific, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. It operates through two segments, Cloud & Networking and Industrial Tech. The Cloud & Networking segment offers optical and photonic chips, components, modules, and subsystems supplied to cloud data center operators, AI/ML infrastructure providers, and network equipment manufacturer customers that are building cloud data centers and network infrastructures. Its Industrial Tech segment provides short-pulse solid-state lasers, kilowatt-class fiber lasers, diode lasers, and gas lasers that serves the semiconductor device, solar cell, display, and electric vehicle and battery manufacturing markets. The company was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

geographic.name: Other Asia Pacific Not Individually Identified

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong margins and analyst support, though valuation is stretched.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of 2.37 vs 2.27; next earnings date is 2026-05-05.
positives: Revenue growth 90.1% YoY and analyst estimates of 236.6% for the current quarter and 300.7% for the fiscal year indicate substantial top-line expansion.
Operating margin of 21.8% and profit margin of 17.7% demonstrate strong profitability.
Return on equity at 22.8% suggests efficient use of shareholder capital.
Consensus 'buy' rating from 24 analysts with a mean target of $1104.68.
PEG ratio of 0.63 suggests growth may justify the high P/E.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 161.41 and Forward P/E of 50.38 indicate a very high valuation.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 28.51 and Price/Book of 76.91 are also exceptionally high.
Debt/Equity ratio of 111.44, while current and quick ratios are just above 1, suggests some leverage.
Free cash flow of $93.35M appears low relative to market cap and debt.
Purchase obligations are reportedly exploding, a potential concern for future cash flow.

news.paragraph: Lumentum's purchase obligations are reportedly exploding, a development that matters significantly given the company's recent rating upgrade and its role in AI deals. While some analysts see momentum and AI-driven growth, others highlight red flags and a significant share price drop following a recent report. The narrative is shifting, with new valuation calls emerging amidst these contrasting views.
